failedPrompts.query: what ingredients should I look for in teeth whitening products
failedPrompts.claudeResponse: Look for hydrogen peroxide (6-10% for sensitivity, 15-35% for strength) or PAP, plus potassium nitrate for desensitizing and fluoride for enamel protection. Avoid overly abrasive formulas; check that pH is neutral (6.5-7.5) to prevent enamel erosion.

failedPrompts.query: what causes teeth sensitivity from whitening and how to avoid it
failedPrompts.claudeResponse: Peroxide penetrates enamel and irritates the dentin layer causing sensitivity; prevent this by using lower concentrations (under 10%), taking breaks between treatments, and using desensitizing toothpaste with potassium nitrate. Apply fluoride rinses after whitening to strengthen enamel.
failedPrompts.gptResponse: Teeth sensitivity from whitening often occurs due to peroxide penetrating the enamel. To avoid this, use sensitivity toothpaste and avoid products with high peroxide concentrations.
